herb_constituents.name: saponins
herb_constituents.desc: Azadirachta indica saponins are a group of bioactive compounds extracted from the Neem tree, exhibiting potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These saponins have been studied for their ability to modulate the immune system and exert anti-cancer activities in various in vitro and in vivo studies. Additionally, they have been found to have neuroprotective effects, making them a subject of interest for potential therapeutic applications.

herb_constituents.name: flavonoids
herb_constituents.desc: Azadirachta indica flavonoids have been found to possess significant antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to their potential health benefits. These flavonoids can help protect against cell damage, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit anti-cancer properties, making them a promising area of research. Studies have also shown that flavonoids in Azadirachta indica may have cardioprotective effects, helping to reduce the risk of heart disease.

herb_constituents.name: terpenoids

herb_constituents.name: quassinoids

herb_constituents.name: alkaloids
herb_constituents.desc: Azadirachta indica alkaloids are a group of compounds found in the neem tree, with the primary alkaloids being azadirachtin, nimbin, and nimandatin. These alkaloids have been shown to exhibit antimalarial, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making them a valuable component of traditional medicine. Additionally, azadirachtin, a key alkaloid in neem oil, has been found to have insecticidal and anti-inflammatory activities.
